programme.title: Kung Fu Master Saves The Day
programme.desc: A dedicated teacher uses empathy to connect with troubled students with difficult pasts. When faced with budget cuts and a potential firing, his students' newfound academic success, fueled by his influence, saves the school and secures his job.

programme.title: Teen Choses Fast Money
programme.desc: Antwan, a struggling teen, is drawn to drug dealing but finds an unlikely mentor in his English teacher, Mr. Singh. Through poetry, Antwan expresses his hardships, winning a competition and choosing a new path over crime.

programme.title: Teacher Faces The Toughest Class
programme.desc: Terrorists hijack Yasmine's plane, but she, a seemingly demure teacher, neutralizes them. Starting a new life teaching unruly high schoolers, Yasmine finds herself entangled in a drug operation tied to a mayoral candidate, forcing her to use her past skills to protect her students.

programme.title: Kung Fu Master Helps Teens In School
programme.desc: Malic, a hardened teen, is trapped in a gang lifestyle by his brother. A caring teacher, Mr. Wang, uncovers Malic's past trauma and helps him find a new path, inspiring Malic to help his brother escape the cycle of violence.

programme.title: Scrawny Teen Becomes Popular
programme.desc: Benji, a bullied teen, learns martial arts for self-defense. He uses his skills in an underground fight club for money, but guided by his master's wisdom, he chooses to fight for justice, helping his mom and a neighbor escape abusive situations.

programme.title: Student Takes IQ Serum To Impress Girl
programme.desc: Chad, a lazy law student, uses a "genius serum" to ace exams and impress a girl. When the serum's effects fade, he faces academic and financial ruin, forcing him to abandon shortcuts and embrace hard work, ultimately finding true success.

programme.title: Mom Doesn't Want Baby With Disability
programme.desc: Fearing her newborn might be blind, Rose secretly swaps her for a healthy infant. Years later, Marcus discovers the truth through a chance encounter and a DNA test. Rose is arrested, and the two families unite to raise both daughters, eventually reconnecting with Rose's estranged, blind mother.

programme.title: Kid Breaks Every Rule to Be Genius
programme.desc: After years of bullying and academic failure, Bernie, a student with dyslexia, solves an "unsolvable" equation. He overcomes his stepfamily's discouragement to earn a full scholarship to Stanford.

programme.title: Students Don't Know Teacher Is Secretly Princess
programme.desc: A hidden princess teaching at an inner-city school is exposed. Though her family tries to force her home, her students' love convinces the Queen to let her stay and become the school's principal.

programme.title: Son Discovers Mom Has Only 30 Days to Live
programme.desc: Drew Binsky, obsessed with travel since childhood, shocks his parents by announcing he will travel the world instead of pursuing a traditional career. After his savings run out, his girlfriend, Deanna, helps him launch a successful vlogging career, eventually paying off his parents' home.

programme.title: Parents Force Kid Into Being Child Star
programme.desc: Greedy adoptive parents exploit 12-year-old Dylan for fame and fortune. After a social worker uncovers their abuse and forgery, they are arrested, allowing Dylan to finally live a normal life.

programme.title: Girl Takes Lip Filler Way Too Far
programme.desc: Insecure about her thin lips, Millie blows her savings on fillers but, unsatisfied, gets a dangerous, "cheap" procedure. After a severe infection, she learns that true beauty comes from within.

programme.title: Teen Caught Shoplifting Red-Handed
programme.desc: In this "Cinderella" twist, nerdy Sheldon is manipulated by popular girl Raven, who uses him for discounts and even frames him for theft. After meeting Raven's boyfriend Brady in jail, the two team up to record her confession. Raven is arrested, and the two former rivals become friends.

programme.title: Stepdad Steals From Daughter
programme.desc: After Jasmine's song goes viral, her mom's boyfriend Mike cons his way into being her manager and gambles away her $125k bonus. He tries to frame her for drugs to get a conservatorship, but Jasmine exposes his fraud in court.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E02

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E03
programme.desc: When Tara's crush on Ethan threatens to derail her friendship with Kat, Sammy gets her to write a pros and cons list to clear her head. As the roommate war escalates, Abigail wins a decisive victory - emailing Tara's list to the whole school.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E04
programme.desc: The first years are given trust exercises to help forge connections with their pas de deux partners. Tara is frustrated when Christian refuses to participate, however they connect unexpectedly when Tara follows him to the beach.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E05
programme.desc: Sammy is forced to choose between dance and his father when compulsory Saturday ballet classes clash with his Synagogue services. Christian struggles to accept the rules of the Academy.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E06
programme.desc: The National Ballet comes to town and Tara is thrilled to learn that Kat's mother is Natasha Willis, the Company's Principal dancer and her childhood idol. Sammy's girlfriend, Mia, also arrives forcing Sammy to evaluate his growing feelings.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E07
programme.desc: Christian reluctantly agrees to dance in Ethan's showcase but can't stand the derivative choreography. In order to show this ballet boy some real hip-hop, Christian takes Ethan home to Maroubra.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E08
programme.desc: Abigail is devastated that her body seems to be developing and defies Miss Raine by wearing a loose top to class. Sammy is forced to buy a dance belt, with Christian as his unlikely shopping buddy.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E09
programme.desc: Mocking her friends' obsession with teen popstar Myles Kelly, Kat creates a video of herself dancing to one of his songs and then posts it on the internet. She is surprised when the record company invites her to dance in Myles's next video clip.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E10
programme.desc: Christian's best mate Aaron comes to stay and reveals the truth about the robbery they committed. Later, Aaron uses Christian as an alibi forcing Christian to lie to the police, and to question whether old friends are necessarily good friends.

programme.title: Dance Academy, Season 1 S01 E11
programme.desc: Tara is excited when Ethan asks her to star in his choreography assessment piece, but Ethan only has eyes for his mentor, Dana. Abigail's sister, Paige, stays for the weekend.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Professor Jeremy Coid, a forensic psychiatrist with 30 years of experience working with the UK's most dangerous killers, gives us a unique insight into the minds of serial murderers, sadists and psychopaths.

programme.title: Minutes With: Episode 139
programme.desc: Josh Lane bravely speaks out about his decade-long pornography addiction, revealing the most difficult moments he faced as a child discovering porn and as an adult who became dependent on it, and his ongoing journey of recovery.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Aged 4, Marina Chapman was kidnapped from her home village in rural Colombia and left in the jungle. She tells us how she learned to survive and communicate with monkeys and how she was eventually brought back into civilisation after several years.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In this week's episode, ex-career criminal John Burton, a.k.a 'Juice Bomb John', recounts how he began a life of crime in the 90's Liverpool rave scene, smuggled millions of pounds worth of black market goods into the UK, and reformed to develop an app helping people find work after leaving the prison system.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In this week's episode, we speak to Jo Millington, a forensic scientist and blood spatter expert on her incredible work identifying murderers through the 'magic art' of crime scene investigation. Jo reveals the common mistakes murderers make when killing, explains how you can never totally clean up a crime scene and how it feels like to catch a 7/7 London bomber.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Actor Adam Pearson grew up with a genetic condition called neurofibromatosis, which causes the growth of tumours on nerve tissues on his face and upper body. Today, he tells us about his youth, coping with bullying and his successful acting career.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Philip Schlaffer, a former member of the German Neo-Nazi movement, tells us about his radicalisation story, what life inside the group was like and how he left the movement and changed his life for the best.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In this episode of Minutes With, former city trader Gary Stevenson reveals more about his extraordinary life making millions as the world's best trader in London. Despite being expelled from school at age 16, Gary persisted and won his dream job in a global bank by mastering a card game, even though it was rigged against him. In this episode, Gary reveals how it feels to win $35 million betting on an economic downturn, the bizarre ways millionaires act on the trading floor and why he walked away from banking to warn the world about rising wealth inequality.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: In this episode of Minutes With, we speak to Jacob Dunne, who accidentally killed a man with one punch on a night out. Jacob tells us how this event changed his life, how it feels to speak to the parents of the victim and how he's moving forward.

programme.title: Minutes With
programme.desc: Chris Fuller, a former British Army Bomb Disposal Officer, who now does humanitarian work in the Middle East, talks to us about defusing bombs in the centre of London, and the difference between working in the UK and abroad.

programme.title: Bewitched S3E87
programme.desc: Finding Samantha struggling to repair a lamp, Aunt Clara uses her uncertain powers of magic to summon an electrician. Benjamin Franklin, in l8th century clothes, appears. Samantha is upset by Ben's presence and Aunt Clara's wail that she can't remember how to undo the spell. Darrin, astonished by Ben's appearance in his home, can't convince Larry Tate that Ben is not is creation for an advertising campaign for Franklin Elec-tronics. Abner Kravitz refuses to believe Gladys' report that the real Ben Franklin is at the Stephens'. Bowing to Samantha's pleas, Darrin agrees to allow her and Aunt Clara to show Ben how his inventions are being used is the city. The next morning Ben, leaving a note that he has decided to tour the city alone to avoid causing domestic discord, sets out. Darrin, Samantha and Aunt Clara start searching for him. At a firehouse, inspecting an old fire engine, Ben steps on the accelerator. The engine shoots out into the street and slams into a curb. Ben is arrested and taken to court, where the judge, sure it is all a publicity stunt, charges him with grand larceny and sets bail at a thousand dollars. Darrin puts up the bail, knowing he will lose it if Ben doesn't appear for trial is a month. Just then, Aunt Clara remembers the spell and Ben vanishes.

programme.title: Khatta Meetha
programme.desc: Sachin Tichkule, an ambitious contractor, faces opposition at home and work. Despite his struggles, he perseveres while family members exploit opportunities for personal gain, testing his resolve.

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.2
programme.desc: Seok Han decides to do the surgery for Tae Soo's mom. Later, Tae Soo joins Taesan University Hospital as a fellow. However, he seems to be always cleaning up after Dong Joon's mess. As such days go by, Hee Dong suddenly orders Tae Soo to send a dying patient to another hospital. Tae Soo decides to do surgery for the patient he was ordered to send away. He manages to succeed with the help from Seok Han. Meanwhile, an emergency occurs during Soo Yeon's way back to Korea. Soo Yeon has no choice but to take the patient to the nearest hospital, but they don't have the equipment and supplies needed.

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.3
programme.desc: Finding himself in a critical situation with no other viable option, Doctor Park Tae Soo convinces Doctor Yoon Soo Yeon to use super glue and a piece of a latex glove to patch a bleeding heart. Miraculously, the makeshift patch stops the bleeding, and the patient is rushed to Taesan University Hospital. Meanwhile, as Tae Soo prepares to go in for surgery, his mother calls to hear his voice. Tae Soo's mother goes into cardiac arrest. Luckily, Doctor Yoon Soo Yeon manages to get her on the ECMO machine and saves her life. With his mother in stable condition, Tae Soo meets with the family members of the patient who suffered the cardiac tamponade and decides to disclose that super glue was used to save his life. Right then, he sees who the uncle is and reconsiders telling the fact.

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.4
programme.desc: Knowing her very existence could have been the reason for Professor Choi not being able to save his daughter's life, Soo Yeon visits Yoo Bin's memorial to show her respect. Tae Soo watches Professor Choi tormented by the memory of losing his daughter and walks away in sympathy. Despite his brother being alive, Hwang Jin Chul shows up to the hospital and threatens Tae Soo for using super glue. Taesan University Hospital director, Yoon Hyun Il, fires Tae Soo after he hears of his super glue procedure. However, his brother, Yoon Hyun Mook, the chief executive, hears about Hyun Il diverting funds from the Africa project and plans to have Hyun Il resign. Meanwhile, Soo Yeon sides with Tae Soo and tries to find a way to save his position at the hospital."

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.5
programme.desc: Blackmailed, Professor Choi Seok Han faces a predicament that could lead him to find out the truth behind his daughter's death. The thought of never finding out what happened torments him and pushes him to the surgery room. Meanwhile, Tae Soo stands on guard and opposes the idea. Tae Soo's mother finally awakes from surgery but still urgently needs a heart transplant. Soo Yeon has difficulty breathing and plans to have surgery done by Professor Choi Seok Han. Meanwhile, Professor Choi Seok Han suffers from knowing the truth about his daughter's death and plans to get his revenge.

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.6
programme.desc: Even with Hyun Il's confession and a plea for forgiveness, Hyun Mok stands firm on his decision to see him leave Taesan University Hospital. After meeting with Hyun Il, Hyun Mok and Soo Yeon get into an accident and are both seriously injured. When they arrive at the hospital, Hyun Mok begs Professor Choi to save Soo Yeon's life once again. The faculty attends Hyun Mok's funeral and starts to gossip. Everyone knows with Hyun Mok dead and Soo Yeon grasping onto her life, Hyun Il will become the hospital's chief director. Moreover, what Professor Choi Seok Han yelled as he tried to save Hyun Mok, starts to echo. Later, a brain-dead patient arrives at the hospital.

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.7
programme.desc: Professor Choi Seok Han asks Tae Soo's mother a very tough question that puts her life on the line. Knowing that her decision will save another doctor's life, she consents to give the heart that was for her to Soo Yeon, who is in critical condition. Tae Soo is heartbroken when he finds out and blames Professor Choi for making the wrong decision. After giving up the heart, Tae Soo's mother condition worsens. Taesan University Hospital inaugurates Yoon Hyun Il as the new director, and Soo Yeon regains her conscience. However, when she wakes up, the sight of her uncle frightens her. Meanwhile, Professor Seok Han prepares a press conference to promote the hospital.

programme.title: Heart Surgeons Ep.8
programme.desc: Tae Soo holds his mother's funeral. The operating team comes to say goodbye and to give Tae Soo support, except for Professor Choi. The newly appointed hospital director, Yoon Hyun Il, has lunch with his staff and later, calls Professor Choi for answers. Meanwhile, Ji Na and Dong Joon argue over who should be taking care of Soo Yeon. A year passes. Soo Yeon fully recovers and becomes an assistant professor at Taesan University Hospital. Professor Choi proposes to perform a new procedure to save fetuses with congenital heart diseases, but Soo Yeon opposes. However, Director Yoon agrees and approves the operation and for Soo Yeon to assist.

programme.title: A Girl Who Can See Smell Ep.13
programme.desc: Cho Rim sees the same scent on Jae Hee she's seen before on Dr. Chun's body. She tells Moo Gak she wants the other detectives to know of her ability. Now knowing this, the special investigation unit sets up a plan to secure evidence from Jae Hee's house.

programme.title: A Girl Who Can See Smell Ep.14
programme.desc: Moo Gak tries to find Lt. Yeom at Jae Hee's house but does not find the secret room. Jae Hee tells Lt. Yeom will give you an autobiography. Cho Rim feels guilty Lt. Yeom was taken in every stead.

programme.title: A Girl Who Can See Smell Ep.15
programme.desc: Jae Hee tries to escape with the autobiographies, but gets arrested by Moo Gak instead. Meanwhile, Cho Rim is starting to recall memories of her parents and her accident.

programme.title: A Girl Who Can See Smell Ep.16
programme.desc: Moo Gak, Lt. Yeom and the other detectives are shocked to see the video of Jae Hee kidnapping Cho Rim. Meanwhile, Cho Rim leaves a clue when she drops her ring on the floor as Jae Hee looks away.

programme.title: Rebirth Ep.1
programme.desc: Seo Ha-eun, a respected detective with no memory of his early childhood, investigates a suspicious motel suicide that may be linked to a case from 20 years ago. As he tracks down a key suspect, the man reacts with shock upon seeing Ha-eun's face.

programme.title: Rebirth Ep.2
programme.desc: Ha-eun becomes convinced the suicide is actually a murder and uncovers ties to a powerful politician, but is ordered to stop the investigation. Meanwhile, hidden connections to a man believed dead 20 years ago begin to surface.

programme.title: Rebirth Ep.3
programme.desc: Ha-eun struggles with haunting dreams and unresolved emotions as he grows closer to Eun-ha. Refusing to close the case, he finds flaws in the suspect's alibi while his partner seeks a crucial witness who may reveal the truth.

programme.title: Namib Ep.10
programme.desc: Unable to trust Soo-hyun, Yoo Jin-woo secretly works a night job at Muse—where his close friend Chris is employed—only to find himself in a dangerous predicament. Meanwhile, Soo-hyun confronts the hidden secret that Jin-woo has been desperately keeping under wraps.

programme.title: Namib Ep.11
programme.desc: Soo-hyun devises a plan to make Yoo Jin-woo the winner of the audition program Star Rise and sell his contract to a major agency, all to fund a factory for her son, Shim Jin-woo, who is about to graduate high school. Meanwhile, her husband Jun-seok expresses deep resentment toward Soo-hyun for her unilateral decision to bring Jin-woo into their lives.

programme.title: Namib Ep.12
programme.desc: A legendary star maker with a Midas touch, Kang Soo-hyun is ousted from her company overnight following a dubious power trip scandal. On that same day, Yoo Jin-woo, a veteran trainee of ten years, is also served a termination notice. Faced with a cold proposal to take Jin-woo in lieu of her severance pay, Soo-hyun falls into a deep dilemma.

programme.title: Honour Ep.1
programme.desc: Meanwhile, Hyun-jin meets with Jun-hyeok, a journalist covering Yu-jung's story, and stumbles upon a chilling lead: a brewing scandal involving a high-stakes prostitution ring.L&J (Listen & Join), a law firm specializing in defending victims of sexual crimes, faces an unexpected crisis in the courtroom while representing Yu-jung, a survivor of sexual assault. During the heat of the trial, Ra-young follows her seasoned instincts and senses that Yu-jung is harboring a dark secret—one that could jeopardize the entire case.

programme.title: Honour Ep.2
programme.desc: Yu-jung appears at the L&J 10th-anniversary ceremony covered in blood. She confesses that reporter Lee Jun-hyeok attempted to sexually assault her and that she killed him while resisting. Hyun-jin, who was the first to witness the scene where Jun-hyeok—who was also her ex-boyfriend—was killed, feels doubt about the incident and begins to dig into the surroundings.

programme.title: Honour Ep.3
programme.desc: As soon as Ra-young obtains evidence that Yu-jung did not kill Jun-hyeok, she is attacked by a mysterious assailant in a green hoodie. L&J is horrified to discover the existence of a group that exploits Yu-jung sexually and even resorts to murder to destroy evidence. They begin to track down a mysterious application.

programme.title: Haechi Ep.10
programme.desc: Ji Pyeong is murdered, and Prince Yeoning is witnessed to be the murderer and it arrested by Saheonbu. He is investigated by Saheonbu but is not willing to speak the truth. Meanwhile, behind the murder cases are what is known as master-murderers and the murderer of Ji Pyeong turns out to be a little girl wearing a tattoo. Moon Soo and Yeo Ji investigate what's behind the murder cases.

programme.title: Haechi Ep.11
programme.desc: The girl belonging to the master murdering group was arrested, and Prince Yeoning is accused of supporting the group. The Patriarchs are putting pressure on the king to have the Crown Prince dethroned. Moon Soo and his colleagues try to stop the Saheonbu from investigating and beating the master murderers but fail. The heads of both parties meet to discuss future matters, and Min Jin Heon is adamant that the nobility runs the kingdom.

programme.title: Haechi Ep.12
programme.desc: Patrolmen find something suspicious while doing their routine patrol. The people praise Prince Yeoning for standing up for them, and the blame naturally falls on the Saheonbu. A cover-up murder case is revealed when Moon Soo is inspecting the neighborhood. Behind the hired murder is Section Chief of Personnel. The king and Prince Yeoning decide to use this as an opportunity for reform.

programme.title: Haechi Ep.13
programme.desc: As Yoon Hyuk takes office, he orders the officials of the three agencies to all be re-evaluated. Meanwhile, the fake appeal for vicarious governing has wreaked havoc in the palace, and the king is suspecting the Crown Prince of treason. Dal Moon, who used to work for the Crown Prince, is moved by his past feelings for Yoon Yeong and starts to work in her favor.

programme.title: Haechi Ep.14
programme.desc: The special judiciary agency is opened to investigate the Crown Prince's involvement in treacherous plottings. Wi Byeong Joo was being interrogated but is released and takes charge of the treason case. Dal Moon is now working for Prince Milpoong. He spreads rumors in the city, saying that the Crown Prince isn't the late king's son. Moon Soo and his colleagues find evidence related to Byeong Joo's coverup of Jeong Seok's murder.

programme.title: Haechi Ep.15
programme.desc: Prince Yeoning persuades Min Jin Heon to side with him for the moment and stops the special judiciary agency. Meanwhile, Dal Moon turns out to have been secretly supporting the Crown Prince by trying to extract information from Prince Milpoong. The Crown Prince visits Prince Milpoong to help Dal Moon get the decisive information. Moon Soo finds evidence for Byeong Joo's murder of Jeong Seok and decides to do a night inspection.

programme.desc: Chile, Puerto Rico, and Japan are some of the locations in the world that receive more UAP sightings than anywhere else. What are the causes behind these so-called “hotspots”.

programme.title: Saltmarshe Hall Part 2
programme.desc: Saltmarshe Hall is a mysterious, haunted house of complete contrasts. Beneath the property lies a network of corridors and cellar rooms and the abandoned North Wing has sat empty for over 70 years,

programme.title: Ye Olde Kings Head
programme.desc: The Great British pub may be on the decline but in some paranormal activity seems to be on the rise and the Ye Olde Kings Head is one of the most haunted.

programme.title: The Tivoli Venue
programme.desc: A well-known venue for accomplished as well as up and coming bands The Tivoli Venue boasts many a ghost. In this episode one of the team gets a warning from the other side that reduces them to tears and something seems to be stalking the team.

programme.title: Tatton Hall
programme.desc: Something about Tatton Old Hall keeps bringing the Most Haunted team back. Something happens that stops two of the team entering, a poltergeist causes confusion and the team get a clear voice from beyond the veil.

programme.title: Fort Paull Part 1
programme.desc: Part 1 of this terrifying place concentrates above ground. The team are alarmed at the results from a new piece of equipment and Yvette meets her match.

programme.title: Fort Paull Part 2
programme.desc: Part 2. The Most Haunted team explore the underground of Fort Paull. With its foundations and footprint dating back 100's of years the team are faced with some terrifying ordeals.

programme.title: Annison Funeral Parlour
programme.desc: Coffins, murder and a malevolent spirit plague the team as they investigate this one-time funeral home. With one member isolated alone another in a coffin the team catch a poltergeist in action

programme.title: Wentworth Woodhouse Part 1
programme.desc: Part 1. The upper floors of Wentworth House are a maze of corridors and rooms and the team have one night to investigate as many as possible. With one whistling spirit, groans and loud noises the team are shaken to their core.

programme.title: The Lost Jewels of Helen of Troy
programme.desc: German adventurer Heinrich Schlieman embarks on a lifelong quest to find the mythical city of Troy and its fabulous treasures.

programme.title: The Hunt for the Book of Spells
programme.desc: The Book of Thoth, a legendary text of magic and wisdom, has inspired Freemasons, Tarot readers, and occultists across centuries. Yet, archaeologists doubt its existence despite its influence on esoteric culture from ancient Egypt to modern times.

programme.title: The Lost City of El Dorado
programme.desc: Legend has it that in the Amazon jungle lies El Dorado, a mythical city of gold. Colonel Percy Fawcett, believed in its existence and spent years searching. In 1925, he vanished with his son and a friend during an expedition revealing a civilization.

programme.title: Manchurian Candidates
programme.desc: Several prominent researchers in the field provide a short history of Mind Control experimentation beginning with the Nazis through to the CIA's infamous MK Ultra program.

programme.title: The Phoenix Lights
programme.desc: On March 13th, 1997, an extraordinary event occurred across the state of Arizona that has become the subject of great debate in the UFO community.

programme.title: Vampires
programme.desc: Could vampires be real? A well-known U.S. paranormal investigator and folklorist in New York City recounts the legend and evolution of the vampire throughout history and from culture to culture.

programme.title: Demonic Possession
programme.desc: The religious practice known as exorcism is examined as well as claims of demonic possession. Richard travels to a Catholic seminary in Yonkers, New York.

programme.title: The Depopulation Agenda
programme.desc: Richard interviews an investigative health reporter and a NY Times best-selling author about their claims that a powerful cabal is waging war on mankind through forced sterilization, vaccination and other means.

programme.title: JFK
programme.desc: Richard meets with some prominent JFK assassination researchers to discuss the November 1963 murder of President John F. Kennedy.

programme.title: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: Remote Viewing
programme.desc: We meet several individuals who claim they have the ability to transcend time and space and identify people, objects, and locations hidden from their view.

programme.title: S2 E3 Mysteries Of The Moon
programme.desc: For thousands of years, mankind has found comfort in its presence.

programme.title: S4 E5 The Hunt for Ringed Planets
programme.desc: Stunning rings of Saturn have mesmerized countless scientists over the centuries.

programme.title: S5 E5 Secrets of the Space Probes
programme.desc: Can space probes find a new Earth...and even make contact with alien life?

programme.title: S4 E12 Extreme Energy
programme.desc: Ours is a universe of energy.

programme.title: S3 E7 Living in Space
programme.desc: How will we live... in space?

programme.title: S2 E7 Astrobiology
programme.desc: Astrobiology is a visionary new science that searches for life in space.

programme.title: S2 E6 Dark Matter
programme.desc: Dark Matter and Dark Energy make up 96% of the Universe.

programme.title: S2 E3 Mysteries Of The Moon
programme.desc: For thousands of years, mankind has found comfort in its presence.

programme.title: Uncharted Amazon
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Peru, a silky anteater is fighting to stay awake and a mother humming bird is struggling to raise her chick. The seasonal downpours are about to begin and the rainforest's animals must strive to cope with the changing conditions. Through millions of years of evolution they have developed bizarre relationships and unexpected strategies to overcome them, but for all their ingenuity, they were never prepared for the arrival of a new species on the scene.

programme.title: If There Was No Land On Earth
programme.desc: The first episode tells about the fascinating annual migration of the Humpback whales to the warm and tranquil waters of Tonga's Vava'u archipelago, where they mate, give birth, and nurture their young. The majesty and intelligence of these magnificent creatures is truly breathtaking. From the playful antics of newborn calves through the haunting songs of adult males to the human impact on their habitats and lives – an inspiring journey.

programme.title: If There Was No Land On Earth
programme.desc: The second episode takes you on an underwater adventure to the remote and beautiful islands of Fiji, where people face the formidable predators of the ocean. Through stunning underwater world teeming with life and intimate interviews with passionate shark feeders we will capture the raw power and beauty of these incredible creatures. We will also shed light on the importance of shark conservation and the urgent need to protect these apex predators from human threats.

programme.title: Our Big Blue Backyard
programme.desc: As the tide rises in New Zealand's Northland Harbours, Stingrays and Eagle rays swim in to feast on shellfish. But they are not the top of the food chain. Only in New Zealand, and only in these harbours do Orca hunt rays. Trapped in the shallows, the rays breach the surface and swim for their lives, as the Orca pursue – with a highly evolved practice for avoiding the rays' stinging tail. As the tide ebbs, the once underwater world of Northland's mangroves surfaces and seethes. Twisted roots that moments ago sheltered fish are now exposed - a seafood platter for patient Herons, Spoonbills, and Shags.

programme.title: Epic Safari Encounters: The Hide Safari Camp Part 2
programme.desc: Kristina continues her stay at the Hide Safari Camp with Sean. Cecil's pride is still in the area scouting for prey. Sean teaches Kristina about elephant anatomy and wild dogs take down an impala and mate right outside camp.

programme.title: Epic Safari Encounters
programme.desc: In this final episode, Kristina shares her favourite highlights, humour or behind the scenes action from each of the 19 safari locations. It has indeed been an epic safari with encounters to remember for a lifetime.

programme.title: Sixteen Legs
programme.desc: Giant prehistoric spiders seek love in the dark. Neil Gaiman journeys into Australia's deepest caves in a real-world “Charlotte's Web” recounting 25 years' study of the first web-spinners. These animals outlasted the dinosaurs, survived the splitting of the continents and have endured the entirety of human civilisation. Award-winning cinematography takes viewers through Tasmania's World Heritage wilderness into an underworld few people have seen.

programme.title: Uncharted Amazon
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Peru, a silky anteater is fighting to stay awake and a mother humming bird is struggling to raise her chick. The seasonal downpours are about to begin and the rainforest's animals must strive to cope with the changing conditions. Through millions of years of evolution they have developed bizarre relationships and unexpected strategies to overcome them, but for all their ingenuity, they were never prepared for the arrival of a new species on the scene.

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Railway Lines
programme.desc: In northern Argentina, the world-famous Tren a las Nubes - the cloud train - crosses dizzying viaducts and deep valleys. At an altitude of over 4000 meters, what was once the highest railway runs past cactus forests and into the driest desert in the world. A technical feat in the Andes and a breathtaking ride through spectacular scenery.In northern Argentina, the world-famous Tren a las Nubes - the cloud train - crosses dizzying viaducts and deep valleys. At an altitude of over 4000 meters, what was once the highest railway runs past cactus forests and into the driest desert in the world. A technical feat in the Andes and a breathtaking ride through spectacular scenery.

programme.title: Women Above the Clouds
programme.desc: Alaska - the largest and at the same time most sparsely populated state in the USA. Only about 740,000 people live here. Often far away in small villages. Almost every village has its own airstrip. Aircrafts are one of the main means of transport in Alaska. Jamie Klaes is one of the many female pilots in the country.

programme.title: Women Above the Clouds
programme.desc: Aerobatics is the supreme discipline in air sports. Only a few pilots dare to navigate a machine during loops, drops and steep flights because nausea and blackouts due to the strong G-forces can be life-threatening. But flight instructor Jenny Holmenes is not deterred - she wants to prove her skills. Although she is still a beginner, she competes in the "Sportsman" class at the Norwegian Aerobatic Championships.

programme.title: Women Above the Clouds
programme.desc: Living conditions are harsh in the Australian outback, especially in William Creek. Only 17 people live here, and in summer even less - only two. There is a motel, a campsite, a pub and the only petrol station in a radius of hundreds of miles. The young bush pilot Hannah Jane Cash lives and works here.

programme.title: Women Above the Clouds
programme.desc: Alia Twal is an enthusiastic pilot, globetrotter and adventurer. She is a first officer with the Jordanian airline Royal Jordanian and flies large passenger planes. She climbs the highest mountains in the world, dives with sharks or goes skydiving. Alia particularly enjoys travelling in her Jordanian homeland.

programme.title: Women Above the Clouds
programme.desc: Nepal is one of the poorest countries in the world. Young women are often disadvantaged here and quite a few are married off before the age of 18. Helicopter pilot Priya Adhikari lives and works here. The 38-year-old is an exception and the only woman in Nepal who ventures into the extremely dangerous heights of the Himalayas in her helicopter.

programme.title: Wild River Rhine
programme.desc: Alluvial forests, roaring waterfalls, steep canyons and sunny slopes flank the river Rhine. The variety of habitats is the reason for the large biodiversity along the stream.There is hardly any other river in Germany that is surrounded by as many myths and legends as the river Rhine. No other river has been the topic of songs, has been painted and travelled equally frequently. Despite this it still seems to be true today what the French poet Victor Hugo once said about this river – the Rhine is a river everybody talks about, nobody explores, everybody visits but no one really knows. The film follows the water against the stream. It starts at the river's delta in the Netherlands, flows through six countries and ends in the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: Germany's Mystic Forest
programme.desc: 2.000 rivers and streams dig deep into the underground and transport their water into reservoirs or "Germany's Wild Amazon", the Wupper. These forests and rivers, together with heathlands and moors, are home to a diverse fauna. Martens, badgers, wild boars, hares, roe deer, and red deer, even black grouse and hazel deer find shelter in these parts. Wolves also have a dominant presence, not to mention those who live underground, such as lizards, bats, and snakes. Accompany us on a journey where we explore every corner of this rugged land. Climb underground into the Bergisches Land, through the vast hidden cave systems that sprawl far beneath the forests. There is also much to be discovered in the water, dive with us in crystal clear streams and rivers with their diverse flora and fauna.

programme.title: Bavaria's Alpine Kingdom
programme.desc: There are sparkling palaces from ice and snow, like those found in a fairytale. The icy castles are the phenomenal work of Mother Nature but man had a hand in the magnificent stone castles of the region. The spectacular and histrionic mountains of the Bavarian Alps challenge wildlife and the people who live there and the rocky ramparts of this mountain kingdom are built of limestone and fossil rocks that tell of its origins beneath the sea. Millions of visitors from all over the world come to Bavaria every year to witness the mysticism of the landscape. Kings and Queens, tourist and locals, all are welcomed in the fantastic alpine kingdom. Early summer often comes with the threat of storms that bring floods that scour the land and threaten many creatures. But farmers risk these dangers to take their herds to the highest pastures, where they can graze on the sweetest flower-filled meadows. And later from the high-quality milk produced by their herds, the famers make highly prized cheese. Humankind lives among eagle owls, chamoix, wild cats, stoats and ravens up in these mountains.

programme.title: Germany's Wild Reservoirs
programme.desc: One of the smallest dams in the Bergisches Land is the Panzertalsperre, with an area of just four-and-a-half football pitches. On the other hand, the Dhünntalsperre is the largest with the area of 660 football pitches. They all transform a stretch of flowing water into still and stagnant water. As a result, completely diverse ecosystems have developed there: where the dipper once dived for insect larvae, cormorants and gray herons now fish and swans and coots cavort. In recent summers, even greater challenges have emerged: the effects of climate change - from extreme drought to destructive floods. On a smaller scale, a rather industrious dam master is operating flood protection: The Beaver. However, the water masses of the Wupper tore away half of his majestic timber castle - and the four young beaver kids were not seen again afterwards. Have they survived the flood? Not everyone can cope with the constant change in the dams, but they still remain an important wonder of engineering for many: for us humans as well as for a number of animals. By combining water management, ecological requirements and tourism a worthwhile goal is possible: Because for the Bergisches Land, its dams are among the most valuable and popular infrastructures that the region has to offer.

programme.title: Mekong Mutilator
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to south east Asia's Mekong river in search of a baby-faced killer.

programme.title: The Alps
programme.desc: From mountain to sea, and from scorching desert to Arctic tundra, Europe's great wilderness contains a stunning and surprising explosion of life. Wildest Europe reveals the incredible beauty and diversity of wildlife in Europe's major natural habitats.

programme.title: Forests
programme.desc: Shrouded in myth and legend, the forests of Germany are world famous, thanks to the fairy tales of the Brothers Grimm. The strange creatures that actually live there are shown in the episode Forests of the four-part series Wild Germany. A lynx out hunting, red deer stags roar to call their harems together, wild boar forage in dead leaves looking for nuts. When one considers how densely populated it is, Germany is home to a surprising number of different species. Around one third of the country is covered in forest. Forests enters a world full of magic and shows the secretive inhabitants you won't see when out for a walk. Spring in the Black Forest brings out the cherry blossom. Not long now, and the fruit used to make the famous Black Forest Gâteau will appear. A doe has deposited its fawn in the high grass under the trees. Nearby, a vixen is on the look-out for food for her cubs. She would just about be able to handle the fawn ... Life in the forest is full of drama. Humans are rarely seen, the animal adventures take place behind a thick curtain of leaves. The wildcat and the lynx have managed to reconquer some regions from which they had long disappeared. For rare animals like black stork and stag beetle, the woods offer a last refuge. The best nature film-makers in Germany spent more than five years travelling the country from the Harz mountains to the Bavarian Forest, from Saxonian Switzerland to the Palatinate Forest, collecting material on the secret lives of forest dwellers.

programme.title: A Tropical Eden: American Jungles
programme.desc: The Neotropical forests of South America extend across huge territories with an incredible diversity of trees and plants.

programme.title: The Birds
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups Nugget and Beaker get their first lesson in desert survival out on the Manor.

programme.title: Between Estonia and Finland
programme.desc: The Baltic Sea is the youngest ocean in the world and first evolved after the last Ice Age. We think we know everything about it, yet still it has many surprises in store. Wind and waves still shape the shores of the Baltic Sea. The second episode presents a world comprising tens of thousands of islands, between Estonia, Gotland in Sweden and the Finnish Archipelago Sea. In the Autumn on Saaremaa, the largest Estonian island, nasal sounds can be heard from one corner to the next: The mating calls of the Moose. Huge bulls make their way to the traditional rutting areas in the south of the island. The landscape here is stony and riddled with countless bays. In the north, the Panga Cliff, a limestone wall of some 20 metres in height and more than 400 million years old. In the beginning of March, animal filmmaker Christoph Hauschild and his team accompany two seal researchers going about their work. Their destinations are two small islands in the Bay of Riga. A popular spot for grey seals. This is the first time that a film team has been allowed on the island. Just how many animals there are, can first be seen from the air. The beach is packed with young seals. The largest gathering in the Baltic Sea. The absence of pack ice forces the mothers to come here. Global warming has visibly changed the Baltic Sea as far as the Bay of Riga. The winters are simply too hot for the well-insulated young seals. So as not to overheat, they take dips in the cooling water. The underwater camera captures intimate moments and reveals just how playfully the youngsters enjoy the cool water. Just 200 kilometres to the west lies an ancient coral reef, Gotland. The Swedish island is like an ark in the central Baltic Sea. Much of the flora and fauna one otherwise only finds in Southern Europe can be found here on the second largest island in the Baltic Sea. Golden eagles breed here in the trees and in the spring, the ground is covered in orchids. 35 different varieties grow on Gotland.

programme.title: Egypt
programme.desc: Wildest Middle East reveals the remotest and most surprising corners of this ancient land, where you will find nothing is as it seems and where the natural world is fighting a constant battle to survive in an ever changing world.

programme.title: Blue Realm
programme.desc: Each year, hundreds of critically endangered manatees are killed in U.S. waters by boats, disease and cold weather. 2006 was the worst year on record for manatee deaths 416 animals perished.

programme.title: Wild Ones
programme.desc: Wild Ones draws inspiration from the rhythms of nature, delving into the wonders of the animal kingdom. This captivating series explores the diverse behaviors that define survival—hunting and feeding strategies, family bonds, defense tactics, and the intricacies of courtship and mating. Who reigns supreme in the wild? Discover the untold stories of nature's fiercest competitors and most fascinating creatures.

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Capuchin Matty needs a visit from the vet after fighting leader Erico for the top spot, and the birth of a baby woolly monkey is captured on camera.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: There's big news for one of the chimp groups, and rescued marmoset Jangles summons up the courage to join her new mate Reggie in the outside enclosure.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Vet Nic Masters uses high-tech diagnostic equipment to examine health issues in some of the park's smaller primates, including capuchin Louis and patas monkey Penelope.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The primate care staff move more than thirty capuchins in a day, the chimps react to a fallen tree and the guenons finally get to try out their revamped home.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Chimp Bryan has a trip to the dentist to remove a rotten molar, and Lee Butler returns to Vietnam to assess repairs needed at Monkey World's sister sanctuary.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison and Jeremy travel to Argentina to meet two chimps as they prepare to bring them back to Dorset. The team attempt to sort out the macaques' hierarchy problems.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison and Jeremy prepare to fly Sasha and Kangoo from Argentina to Dorset, which means crating and loading them in the middle of the night.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Dr Alison Cronin and animal manager Jeremy return from a trip to Argentina with Sasha and Kangoo, two chimpanzees who are given a new home at the park, and Tuan's orangutans receive a treat.

programme.title: On the Trails of the Glaciers
programme.desc: Glaciers are found in Alaska at low altitudes, passing through the long valleys before they arrive at the sea.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Centuries of pollution despoiled New York Harbor, turning it into a virtual dead zone. But now, the most ambitious restoration project in any city on Earth aims to bring it back to life.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: In southeast Florida, conservationists are out at dawn every morning, surveying and protecting the nests of three turtle species to keep them safe from human traffic.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Crystal River has long been a safe haven for the Florida manatee, but when an invasive algae wiped out the eelgrass that manatees need for food, the community rallied to restore the river and save the animals that call it home.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Coral reefs around the world are threatened by rising ocean temperatures, but hope is growing off the coast of Hawaii. There, researchers at the Coral Resilience Lab selectively breed corals to withstand ever-increasing amounts of heat stress.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: A decade after the largest dam removal in history, scientists are chronicling a story of ecological rebirth

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: On Panama's remote Pearl Islands, marine biologist Callie Veelenturf is using her research on endangered sea turtles to help the country enshrine the rights of nature into law—and help the communities living there protect their own environment and livelihood.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Farmers grow coffee to save a rainforest that's crucial to local communities and wildlife.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Australian scientists enlist conservation dogs to save threatened native species.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: In the middle of Rio de Janeiro sits the world's largest urban rainforest: Tijuca National park. To combat a century of deforestation and hunting, a team of researchers are repairing the forest's forgotten web of life, one species at a time.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: In Brazil's Atlantic Forest, Mauricio Ruiz has turned his love for nature into action by working with the community to reforest a critical stretch of the nation's most endangered forest, and by using drones to help him reach his goal of planting 15 million trees.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: Fernanda Abra leads a crucial conservation initiative along the roadways of Brazil, where vehicles annually kill about 475 million vertebrates - more than double the country's human population.

programme.title: Wild Hope

programme.title: Thailand

programme.title: Tangier
programme.desc: Tony explores what Burroughs called "Interzone," where artists sought escape from Western moral prohibitions and the possibilities of great empty spaces. But what is it like now?

programme.title: Spain
programme.desc: Tony visits Andalucia during Semana Santa – Holy Week – a time filled with great pageantry and excitement. Tony's longtime DP, Zach Zamboni, currently lives in Granada with his fiancée, Fuen Sanchez.

programme.title: South Korea
programme.desc: After eight years, Tony returns to South Korea. And this time, he's no stranger to kimchi. But just as Tony has changed, so has Korea.

programme.title: Sicily
programme.desc: Sicily isn't about fast food or the fast life. It's about savoring family, life, food and making the most of what you have. Tony will go in search of those foods and their producers and eat his way around the island.

programme.title: South Africa
programme.desc: Once Johannesburg was the most dangerous city in the world, but the end of the apartheid has lead to vast changes in the city. Tony visits the Johannesburg suburb of Hillbrow, and discovers the culture and, of course food.

programme.title: Congo
programme.desc: Bourdain looks at the Congo as one of the more industrialized powers in Africa now largely crumbled to nothingness and chaos with Tim Butcher author of Blood River.

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: Today, Jamie is up with his surprise itinerary in Arizona - taking in the Grand Canyon, Tucson, Tombstone and many other adventures in between... But how will the trip go down with the others?!

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: For this episode, Ben's planned an EPIC 48 hours in Utah, starting in Salt Lake City before venturing down to Zion National Park - but what will the other guys make of his itinerary?

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: This time, Mike's planned 48 hours in New Mexico... And he's determined to show the guys as much of it as possible!! From the White Sands National Park, to Hatch, Santa Fe, Albuquerque and Taos! How do you think he'll get on??!

programme.title: Four Corners
programme.desc: Finishing their Four Corners adventure, it's Barry's turn, and he's not exactly known for his organization skills! Watch as he takes the friends from Grand Junction to the Grand Mesa and on to Vail and Boulder... How will the guys judge him, and who will be the overall winner?!

programme.title: Love On the Rocks
programme.desc: The couple head to southwestern Oklahoma to climb Quartz Mountain but when Jason's confidence is shaken during a failed slab climb, Tayler realizes she may need to prepare to carry a larger load on their journey out west.

programme.title: Love On the Rocks
programme.desc: Failed rock climbing, failed training...struggling marriage? When everything seems to have gone wrong on their road trip in Oklahoma, Tayler and Jason are faced with the question..."are we biting off more than we can chew?" as they head to Wyoming.

programme.title: Love On the Rocks
programme.desc: Jason has attempted to climb the Grand Teton, located in Wyoming, three times before the couple's adventure out west. Will the fourth time be a charm? Or will life's unplanned moments present even greater challenges than Jason anticipated?

programme.title: Chef Swap At The Beach
programme.desc: It's time to bring on the meats. Chef Mason tastes Meat Appetizer Duos from Chef Terry Flores of Rivertown Bistro and Chef Justin Feasel of Fire and Smoke. Both chefs go above and beyond to wow the judges with their carnivorous specials.

programme.title: Chef Swap At The Beach
programme.desc: Chef Jarrett King of O.A.K. Prime and Chef Dan Sheppard of Inlet Prohibition are ready to trade restaurants and wow our judges with their take on an entree with a spicy side. Which chef brought enough heat to win the Chef Swap Knife?

programme.title: Chef Swap At The Beach
programme.desc: Steak and Eggs are a breakfast staple, but this hearty combination certainly doesn't have to be boring. Chef Mason pushes the limit of this classic dish as he challenges Chef Derick Carrozza of Filet's and Chef Casey Blake of Drunken Jack's to both bring their “A-game” in each other's kitchens — or should we say their "egg-game"?

programme.title: Q: Alabama's Barbecue Legends
programme.desc: The story of Alabama barbecue can't be told in sentences. It's told through whiffs of smoke, buckets of sauce and friendly nods across the counter. See how a simple dish has become a culinary anthem for Alabama.

programme.title: Love On the Rocks
programme.desc: A romantic trip to Colorado takes a windy turn when Jason pushes Tayler, despite her hesitation, to summit Mt. Democrat in 70 mph winds and -19 degree weather.

programme.title: Dingomaro
programme.desc: Iranian musician Hamid embarks on a journey through Hormozgan to unite black artists for a concert.

programme.title: Amazing Iran
programme.desc: For the Iranian Turkmen a wedding is one of most important social events in their culture and is still celebrated according to old nomadic tradition. The Turkmen Steppe, the country of a thousand mountains, is a tribute to the hidden treasure that is Iran. Home to a proud nation with a majestic history, we show the freedom of a nomadic life, encounter the wild nature of the people and discover old traditions like the Turkmen wedding. Breathtaking sceneries create a mysticism that transports viewers into the realm of divinity while exposing a world caught between tradition and modernity - nomadism and sedentarism. Weddings in Iran are primarily arrangements between families and the product of lengthy negotiations. Even though many customs are purely symbolic, they still reflect the great significance as a rite of transition for the newlyweds and their families. The life of the Turkmen housewife revolves around weddings – first her own, later those of her children. Raising healthy, capable daughters and sons searching for possible partners mediating contact negotiating each betrothal with the family of the intended shouldering the wedding arrangements and the observance of customs and traditions – the responsibility for all of that falls to the mother. Though the Turkmen daughters and sons of today do insist on having their say, love, affection, trust and the like are still not the decisive criteria in making a match. The main consideration is not the subjective happiness of the couple but the happiness, stability and honor of each of their families as a whole. So it is still not uncommon for the bride and groom to be virtual strangers on their wedding day. The nuptials as a ritual of transition mark a series of profound changes – especially in the life of the bride. Once married, she leaves her family and her home village to take her new place within the family of the groom.

programme.title: Amazing Isfahan
programme.sub-title: Paradise in the Heart of Iran
programme.desc: Take a look at this natural beauty. Isfahan – the Florence of Persia, the blue pearl of Iran. The city in the heart of Iran is the most charismatic city in the country and often uttered in the same breath as Bagdad, Constantinople or Cairo. The former capital of the Persian Safavid Empire has long since lost the status of cosmopolitan city, but it still radiates a cosmopolitan charm of which the inhabitants are extremely proud. With its Iranian and Persian national consciousness, Isfahan emits a form of comeliness that attracts people near and far. Following the 30-year-old carpet dealer Shanty as he tries to establish a business for himself and his fiancée Nina, we discover the soul of the city with mesmeric shots, intimate portraits and true to life portrayals of daily life. For 120 years his family has been dealing with noble Persian carpets, but Shanty dreams of a modern coffee shop, driven by a youthful eagerness to experiment and with passion for the new. In the cozy little espresso bar on the city's famous Imam Square Shanty's barista conjures up the best créma in Isfahan while Pink Floyd, The Grateful Death and Led Zeppelin boom from the loudspeakers nothing new in Berlin or Paris but almost revolutionary here. In intimate pictures, we accompany the youngest son of an old Isfahan family through his bulging everyday life and see how many young Iranians try to unite the culture and traditions of their country with the present.

programme.title: Strongman Champions League

programme.title: Sweet Dreams
programme.desc: "Sweet Dreams" is a soothing show with gentle lullabies and serene animations. It helps kids relax and fall asleep with ease, making bedtime an enjoyable and comforting experience for the whole family.

programme.title: Animal Sounds
programme.desc: This show blends fun and learning by introducing kids to a musical world where animals take center stage. With catchy tunes, children explore songs about pets and wildlife, fostering care and empathy while enjoying an universe of music and adventure!

programme.title: Let's Play Everyday
programme.desc: This show features nursery songs perfect for any moment, offering a fun and engaging soundtrack for kids' daily adventures. These entertaining and educational tunes captivate young audiences, sparking joy and learning, no matter the activity!

programme.title: Jump for Joy
programme.desc: This show helps kids develop motor skills with fun morning exercise songs, boosting health and happiness. The lively lyrics inspire kids and parents to stay active, making mornings a joyful and healthy start for the whole family!

programme.title: Good Morning, Sunshine!
programme.desc: This show teaches kids valuable habits through songs, guiding them through morning routines like waking up, brushing teeth, eating breakfast, and heading to kindergarten. It's a delightful blend of learning and fun!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance an up and down dance. They watch some children painting pictures and patterns on to eggs. In Teletubbyland, Laa-Laa dances with her ball until the magical tree appears.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy show us how to make a Turban. The Tubbies go for a walk, but Po, Laa-Laa and Dipsy want to play with their favourite things.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and teaches the Teletubbies a nursery rhyme. Then they watch Becky go for a ride on a Tram. Po rides her scooter too fast and knocks the other Tubbies over.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies watch some children are playing football in the park. Laa-Laa and Po love playing with the ball. While they play they sing a song about the ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind a hill to watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty sausage. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Po go for a walk, so Laa-Laa decides to dance a secret dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number three. Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa go for a walk. They count three trees and three flowers and Po follows behind counting too.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are asleep when a voice trumpet appears singing the rhyme 'Frére Jacques'. The Teletubbies laugh and call "Again, again!"

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies see a little girl and her brother who love salad. They go shopping in a market a market with their grandma to buy some salad to eat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two boys make Chinese Lanterns with their uncle. In Teletubbyland, it is time for Tubby Toast, but where has Laa-Laa's toast gone?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies and some children learn about the colour blue. In Teletubbyland Po is watching the blue sky when it begins to rain.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Noo-Noo tidies up Home hill before the Teletubbies slide up the slide to watch Natalie tell us about her brother's christening.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch Sarah and Mark give their sheep a winter-feed. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies cannot decide whether to have Tubby Custard or Tubby Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies leave their favourite things to watch some boys spinning some spinning tops to celebrate the Hanukah festival. Tinky Winky wears the skirt on his head to do a very special round and round dance.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry, Cherry and the girls form a new band, Strawberry and the Sweet Beats, and prepare to go on the road to perform at the Opening Ceremony of the Berry Big City Games! While master mechanic Berrykin Bruce turns Cherry's tour bus into the band's hotel-on-wheels, Strawberry and her friends pack for the big trip. But the rest of Berry Bitty City points out that someone needs to run the girls' businesses while they are gone.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's the last week of summer and Berry Bitty City is hopping with activity as the girls prepare for their tropical island vacation at Berry Breezy Bay Resort. But just as they're about to leave, bad news: the resort is closed for the season due to berry breezy weather. The girls are disappointed, but Orange isn't giving up. She says if they can't go to the tropical island, maybe they can bring the tropical island here!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With only a few days of summer left, the girls are about to enjoy their first vacation in their newly-created, tropical beach resort at Berry Bitty Lake, when who should descend upon them but Mavis Maraschino, the comically over-the-top "Entertainment Tonight"-style show host. Mavis has dumped her focus on fashions in favor of an even glitzier show called Lifestyles of the Berry Big Bitty Stars, and she wants her first segment to be about how pop superstar Cherry Jam spends her vacation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: On a beautiful winter's night, Blueberry reads the Baby Berrykins a story of what happened one winter in Berry Bitty City-a winter that almost wasn't. Just as everyone is ready for winter fun and fashions, a sudden warming melts the snow and ruins their plans. Improvising with snowmen made of shaved ice and cotton puffs, Strawberry and her friends play "Capture the Snowman's Carrot Nose." The Berrykins want to join in the fun, so the girls play against them, with Strawberry refereeing.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's another windy winter day, and the postal bees are staying indoors-the wind is too strong for their berry bitty wings. "We don't know when the next mail will get through!" It's bad news for Blueberry, who has been anxiously awaiting the next installment of a Patty Persimmon Detective story. If only she could get to the post office in Berry Bitty Dale where her package is waiting!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry, Cherry and the girls form a new band, Strawberry and the Sweet Beats, and prepare to go on the road to perform at the Opening Ceremony of the Berry Big City Games! While master mechanic Berrykin Bruce turns Cherry's tour bus into the band's hotel-on-wheels, Strawberry and her friends pack for the big trip. But the rest of Berry Bitty City points out that someone needs to run the girls' businesses while they are gone.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Berrykins Ed and Earl report to Princess Berrykin and Strawberry that they are certain there is a monster in the berry patch! They saw something with many noses and many tails making weird noises in the bushes. But upon further investigation by Strawberry and her friends, it is discovered that friendly boy Huckleberry Pie was driving his pet-mobile of orphan dogs through the countryside, took a wrong turn and got stuck near Berry Bitty City.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Blueberry is disappointed in the girls' lack of enthusiasm for the Patti Persimmon mystery novels, her favorite book series. She finds herself missing the company of her fellow Persimmon fan Huckleberry, and thinks up a great way to get him to visit. She invites Huck to co-write a story with her to submit to an online mystery magazine.. Huck eagerly accepts and comes for a visit.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest she wants to fit in and contribute to the community.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With only a few days of summer left, the girls are about to enjoy their first vacation in their newly-created, tropical beach resort at Berry Bitty Lake, when who should descend upon them but Mavis Maraschino, the comically over-the-top "Entertainment Tonight"-style show host. Mavis has dumped her focus on fashions in favor of an even glitzier show called Lifestyles of the Berry Big Bitty Stars, and she wants her first segment to be about how pop superstar Cherry Jam spends her vacation.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Disappearing Dog Show
programme.desc: When Huckleberry and Blueberry's Patti Persimmon-style mystery story is rejected by an online mystery magazine, Blueberry and Huck have to come up with a new way to raise support for Huckleberry's Hotel for Homeless Pets back in Berry Big City. Strawberry suggests they hold a big dog show! Plum makes over-ambitious plans to display talents that her dog does not quite have, trying to teach him complicated dance routines.
